text: shelter during the storm, is the only reason I’m standing here now .” She turned and met Newton’s gaze. The plates over his eyes shifted subtly, and Lara nodded at him . After a moment’s hesitation, he stepped forward, pulling down his hood and removing the cloth from his lower face. The people gasped, their quiet conversations fueled with new energy . “He reactivated Ronin, and took us to a place where we could find help. These people—” Lara gestured at the soldiers “—saved us. They fed me, tended our wounds, gave us shelter. And now, they’re here to help .” She pointed in the direction of the wall, the top of which was visible over the shacks. “As we speak, there’s another team going in there. They’re going to start the fight, but it’s up to us to rally together to finish it. Together, we will march into the Market, and Newton will convince the bots to join us .” The faces in the crowd were a variety of conflicting emotions — hope, fear, doubt, determination . “We can only do it if we’re all together. As one, we can defeat him. We can take back this city and live .” “What the hell is going on here?” a familiar voice called . Lara clenched her jaw as Devon pushed his way to the front of the crowd. She hadn’t spoken to him since the day Ronin took her to the Bot District . He looked the same as she remembered, but her experience had altered her perspective. Beneath the dirt smudges on his cheeks and the overly-large rags over his clothes, he was a well-built man with a filled-out face and a healthy color to his skin. A man who always had plenty of food. A man who always avoided the gearheads despite the attention his wealth should have brought . “We’re taking down Warlord,” she said . Devon’s stride faltered and his eyes widened before he burst into laughter. He doubled over, hand on his stomach, guffawing. Lara — and the entire crowd — stood silent as he wandered closer to her, eyes filled with mirth . “What can you do? Besides fucking bots, I mean.” He stroked his crotch. “You didn’t need to run off with one of them if you needed it so badly .” “We don’t have time for this shit, Devon.” She shifted her attention to the others. “If you want to be free of Warlord, stand with us. Anyone too weak to fight will be taken to a safe location where — ” “You’re fucking serious.” Devon furrowed his brow and turned to the crowd. “You believe this? Follow this bot-banger, and you know what’ll happen. You’ll all get killed .” “Those men are soldiers. Look at their guns,” someone said . “Warlord’s got guns, too,” Devon replied. “And even without them, one of his bots could tear any of us apart with its bare hands .” “What other choice do we have?” another person demanded . “Go home and keep living !” “Easy for you to say.” Steve glared at Devon. “How is it you got so much, boy? How is it they ain’t come knocking on your door yet ?” Devon shrugged. “Not my fault if you aren’t resourceful enough to get what you need without digging through trash .” Lara’s breath caught in her throat. With his words, everything fell into place. No one got by without trading in this world. For most of them, that meant scavenging, scraping together meager crop harvests, or putting their unique talents to use — shoemaking or tailoring or making pottery. She’d never seen Devon work. Never saw him hide when the gearheads came around. And they always seemed to know exactly who to look for, who to punish . “It was you ,” she said . He looked over his shoulder at her, frowning . “ What ?” Lara stepped forward, and, as he turned to face her, swung her arm. Her fist connected with his mouth, and his lips mashed against his teeth. The pain in her hand was distant, muted by her rage. His head snapped to the side and he tumbled into the dirt. A small cloud of dust rose around him . “It was you all along, you son of a bitch !” Devon pushed himself up on hands and knees, head down, and spat blood. Glancing up, he met her eyes. He held her gaze as he slowly stood, blood trickling down his chin from his split lips. His hands fell to his sides. “Best watch what you say, bot slut .” “Or what? You going to tell the gearheads, like you told them about Kate ?” A collective gasp washed over the crowd . “What?” Gary asked, moving closer . “Devon is Warlord’s rat. How else would he live so easy, while they always know who breaks the rules ?” “Is it true?” a young woman asked. Her face was pale beneath a layer of grim, and the two smallest fingers of her right hand were gone. Lara recognized her as one of Devon’s past lovers . “Are you going to believe this bitch? This fucking traitor ?” “Only one traitor here,” Steve said, crossing his arms over his chest, his steely glare fixed on Devon . Gary growled. The sound grew into a bellow of rage as he charged Devon, tackling the man to the ground. They rolled, Gary punching wildly. Devon forced a boot between them, kicking Gary backward, and scrambled to his feet. He drew a knife from his belt and turned his wild-eyed gaze to Lara . “You fucking bitch !” Before he advanced a single step, the soldiers placed themselves in front of Lara, rifles raised . “Drop your weapon,” Cooper commanded . “Don’t shoot him,” she said. “He’s not worth throwing our chance away .” Devon halted, staring at the barrel of Cooper’s gun. Steve lunged forward, grasping Devon’s arm
